name: CCC Data Intelligence Upgrade
description: Capture uncaptured gold mine data from antigravity.db, supermemory.db, and 12 JSONL files into claude.db. Add Research, Cost, and Pipeline Health dashboard tabs. Eliminate silent pipeline failures.

userStories.title: Cost Tracking Table + Backfill
userStories.description: As a dashboard user, I want to see actual API cost data so that I can track spend trends and ROI accurately.
acceptanceCriteria: Create cost_events table in config/schema.sql: (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, timestamp INTEGER, model TEXT, input_tokens INTEGER, output_tokens INTEGER, cache_tokens INTEGER, cost_usd REAL, session_id TEXT)
Add cost backfill function to fix-data-flows.py reading from cost-tracking.jsonl (2,757 entries)
Backfill is incremental — only inserts entries newer than MAX(timestamp)
Add /api/cost-detail endpoint returning daily cost breakdown by model
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM cost_events > 2000 after backfill
python3 -m pytest tests/ -v passes
ruff check scripts/ tests/ passes
userStories.notes: cost-tracking.jsonl has fields: ts, model, input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_read, cost_usd. Located at ~/.claude/data/cost-tracking.jsonl

userStories.title: DQ Score History Table + Backfill
userStories.description: As a dashboard user, I want full DQ scoring history so that I can see routing quality trends over time.
acceptanceCriteria: Create dq_score_events table: (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, timestamp INTEGER, query_hash TEXT, model TEXT, dq_score REAL, validity REAL, specificity REAL, correctness REAL, complexity REAL, routing_method TEXT)
Backfill from ~/.claude/kernel/dq-scores.jsonl (4,940 entries)
Backfill from antigravity.db dq_scores table (1,620 entries), dedup by timestamp
Incremental — only inserts entries newer than MAX(timestamp)
Add /api/dq-history endpoint with daily averages and trend data
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM dq_score_events > 4000 after backfill
python3 -m pytest tests/ -v passes
ruff check scripts/ tests/ passes
userStories.notes: dq-scores.jsonl fields: timestamp, dq_score, validity, specificity, correctness, model, complexity, method. antigravity.db path: ~/.agent-core/storage/antigravity.db

userStories.title: Research Intelligence Tables + Backfill
userStories.description: As a dashboard user, I want to see my research corpus (papers, findings, URLs) so that I can track knowledge accumulation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create research_findings table: (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, timestamp INTEGER, content TEXT, source TEXT, tier INTEGER, category TEXT, session_id TEXT, quality_score REAL)
Create research_papers table: (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, arxiv_id TEXT UNIQUE, title TEXT, relevance_score REAL, added_at INTEGER, category TEXT)
Backfill findings from antigravity.db findings table (33,240 rows) using ATTACH DATABASE
Backfill papers from antigravity.db papers table (1,424 rows)
Add /api/research endpoint returning totals, top 10 papers by relevance, recent findings
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM research_findings > 30000
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM research_papers > 1000
python3 -m pytest tests/ -v passes
ruff check scripts/ tests/ passes
userStories.notes: antigravity.db path: ~/.agent-core/storage/antigravity.db. Open with read-only connection and short timeout. findings table has: id, content, source, tier, category, session_id, quality, created_at. papers has: id, arxiv_id, title, relevance, category.

userStories.title: Knowledge & Memory Stats Table + Backfill
userStories.description: As a dashboard user, I want to see my knowledge graph stats so that I can track intellectual capital growth.
acceptanceCriteria: Create knowledge_stats table: (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, date TEXT UNIQUE, memory_items INTEGER, learnings INTEGER, memory_links INTEGER, error_patterns INTEGER, reviews_due INTEGER)
Backfill by querying supermemory.db for current counts: memory_items (6,857), learnings (1,893), COUNT of memory_links (12.1M)
Add /api/knowledge endpoint with current totals and growth trend
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM knowledge_stats >= 1 with accurate counts
python3 -m pytest tests/ -v passes
ruff check scripts/ tests/ passes
userStories.notes: supermemory.db path: ~/.claude/memory/supermemory.db. memory_links has 12.1M rows — only capture COUNT(*), never SELECT *. Use read-only connection.

userStories.title: Effectiveness & Co-Evolution Backfill
userStories.description: As a dashboard user, I want to see co-evolution effectiveness scores and applied modifications.
acceptanceCriteria: Create effectiveness_events table: (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, timestamp INTEGER, metric TEXT, value REAL, context TEXT)
Create modification_events table: (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, timestamp INTEGER, mod_type TEXT, target TEXT, status TEXT, confidence REAL)
Backfill from ~/.claude/kernel/effectiveness.jsonl (7,905 entries)
Backfill from ~/.claude/kernel/modifications.jsonl (623 entries)
Add /api/coevolution endpoint with effectiveness trend and recent modifications
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM effectiveness_events > 7000
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM modification_events > 500
python3 -m pytest tests/ -v passes
ruff check scripts/ tests/ passes
userStories.notes: effectiveness.jsonl fields vary — extract timestamp + metric name + numeric value. modifications.jsonl has: timestamp, type, target, status, confidence.

userStories.title: Error Catalog Table + Backfill
userStories.description: As a dashboard user, I want to see error patterns and resolution status for system reliability tracking.
acceptanceCriteria: Create error_catalog table: (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, timestamp INTEGER, error_type TEXT, message TEXT, context TEXT, resolved INTEGER DEFAULT 0, resolution TEXT)
Backfill from ~/.claude/data/errors.jsonl (373 entries)
Backfill from antigravity.db error_patterns (39 rows)
Backfill from supermemory.db error_patterns (8 rows), dedup by error type
Add /api/errors endpoint with error frequency, resolution rate, top patterns
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM error_catalog > 350
python3 -m pytest tests/ -v passes
ruff check scripts/ tests/ passes
userStories.notes: errors.jsonl has: ts, type, message, context. antigravity.db error_patterns has: pattern, count, last_seen, fix. supermemory.db error_patterns has: pattern, solution, seen_count.

userStories.title: Silent Failure Elimination — Pipeline Health Logging
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want all data pipeline failures logged visibly so that 40-day invisible outages never happen again.
acceptanceCriteria: Create pipeline_health table: (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, timestamp INTEGER, pipeline_name TEXT, status TEXT, rows_processed INTEGER, error_message TEXT, duration_ms INTEGER)
Wrap every fix-data-flows.py function in try/except that logs to pipeline_health on BOTH success AND failure
Replace all bare 'except: pass' in fix-data-flows.py with 'except Exception as e:' that logs the error
Add /api/pipeline-health endpoint showing last run status per pipeline step
Add 3 tests: pipeline_health populated on success, error logging works, endpoint returns valid JSON
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT pipeline_name) FROM pipeline_health >= 8
python3 -m pytest tests/ -v passes
ruff check scripts/ tests/ passes
userStories.notes: fix-data-flows.py is at ~/.claude/scripts/fix-data-flows.py (outside git repo). The CCC repo copy at scripts/ccc-api-server.py serves the API. Pipeline names should match function names: fix_command_events, fix_self_heal_events, etc.

userStories.title: Daemon Health Table + API Endpoint
userStories.description: As a dashboard user, I want to see which daemons are running, crashed, or stale in the dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Create daemon_health table: (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, timestamp INTEGER, daemon_name TEXT, status TEXT, pid INTEGER, exit_code INTEGER)
Add daemon health check to fix-data-flows.py: parse 'launchctl list | grep com.claude' and write results to daemon_health
Add /api/daemons endpoint returning current daemon states with PIDs and status
SSE heartbeat events include daemon count and any unhealthy daemons
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM daemon_health > 0 with accurate states
python3 -m pytest tests/ -v passes
ruff check scripts/ tests/ passes
userStories.notes: 13 Claude LaunchAgents exist. Parse launchctl output format: 'PID\tExitCode\tLabel'. PID '-' means not running. Exit code -15 or -9 means crash.

userStories.title: Research Intelligence Dashboard Tab (Tab 18)
userStories.description: As a dashboard user, I want a Research tab showing papers, findings, and knowledge growth.
acceptanceCriteria: Add tab 18 'Research' to dashboard HTML with keyboard shortcut
Display total findings count, total papers, top 10 papers by relevance score
Chart.js line chart: findings accumulation over time (monthly buckets)
Chart.js bar chart: papers by category
Data sourced from /api/research endpoint
Take Chrome DevTools screenshot saved to assets/tabs/18-research.png
python3 -m pytest tests/ -v passes
ruff check scripts/ tests/ passes
userStories.notes: Follow existing tab pattern in dashboard HTML. Use Chart.js (already loaded). Match dark theme styling of existing tabs.

userStories.title: Cost Intelligence Dashboard Tab (Tab 19)
userStories.description: As a dashboard user, I want a Cost tab showing actual spend, cost per session, and model efficiency.
acceptanceCriteria: Add tab 19 'Cost Detail' to dashboard HTML with keyboard shortcut
Chart.js line chart: daily cost trend over last 30 days
Chart.js stacked bar: cost by model (opus/sonnet/haiku)
Display: cost per message, predicted monthly spend, comparison to $200/day subscription
Data sourced from /api/cost-detail endpoint
Take Chrome DevTools screenshot saved to assets/tabs/19-cost.png
python3 -m pytest tests/ -v passes
ruff check scripts/ tests/ passes
userStories.notes: The existing Cost tab shows subscription ROI. This new tab shows granular actual spend from cost_events. Different perspective, both valuable.

userStories.title: Pipeline + Daemon Health in Infrastructure Tab
userStories.description: As a dashboard user, I want the Infrastructure tab to show pipeline and daemon health status.
acceptanceCriteria: Add pipeline health section to existing Infrastructure tab (tab 15)
Show last run time per pipeline, status indicator (green/yellow/red), rows processed
Show daemon fleet status table with name, PID, status, exit code
Red indicator if any pipeline failed in last 24 hours or any critical daemon is down
Data sourced from /api/pipeline-health and /api/daemons endpoints
Take Chrome DevTools screenshot to verify
python3 -m pytest tests/ -v passes
ruff check scripts/ tests/ passes
userStories.notes: Infrastructure tab already exists. Add sections below existing content. Critical daemons: api-server, dashboard-refresh, self-heal, supermemory.

userStories.title: Schema Migration System
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a simple migration system so that table changes are applied automatically on startup.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/migrate.py that reads config/migrations/ directory
Each migration is a numbered .sql file: 001_add_cost_events.sql, 002_add_dq_score_events.sql, etc.
Track applied migrations in metadata table (already exists): key='migration_NNN', value='applied', updated_at=timestamp
migrate.py runs idempotently — safe to run multiple times
Create migration .sql files for ALL new tables from US-001 through US-008 (8 files minimum)
Add test: verify migrations apply idempotently (run twice, no errors)
Add test: verify all new tables exist after migration
python3 -m pytest tests/ -v passes
ruff check scripts/ tests/ passes
userStories.notes: Keep it simple — stdlib only. Read .sql files sorted by name, check metadata table for applied, execute if not applied. No rollback needed (we use IF NOT EXISTS in all CREATE statements).
